mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the correlation flips positive during low-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending consumer spending weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of elevated inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which caps equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 and INDPRO rises, or the conditional Rank IC drops below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 250 indicates entrenched inflation that erodes real corporate margins and forces prolonged monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and falling unemployment signals robust consumer demand outpacing labor market slack, which boosts near-term earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and UNRATE decline fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series models, or 5-day returns are negative in > 60% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production levels indicate expanding manufacturing output and stronger business investment, which directly translates to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes exhibit a Rank IC below 0.02 against 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows, or the relationship reverses during supply-constrained periods.
